Navajo: ’Akwii shichei ńt’éé’ nihee hajoodzíí’

English: My cousin followed me.
Navajo: Shił naa’aash dó’ t’áá shikéé’ joolwoł

English: That is the way my grandfather drove me about and spoke to me.
Navajo: Shichei yę́ę ’ákót’éego t’áá nashineeyood dóó t’áá shich’į’ yááłti’

English: So then my grandfather, the man I spoke of as Big Mexican Clansman, volunteered to join him

English: On the next day, which was February 5, we met with the Governor of Arizona.

English: On February 2, four of us set out from Tuba City.

English: On Monday the seventh I went again.
Navajo: Damį́įgo biiskání tsoots’idgóó yoołkáałgo ’índa ’atah ch’ínáánísdzá

English: Then we took off
Navajo: ’Aadóó nihił dah da’diit’a’

Navajo: ’Íídą́ą́’ ’índa t’áá ła’ajį́ dį́įdi ’ałnánéiikaigo ’i’íí’ą́

English: Nonetheless we hauled two loads again by the time the sun went down
Navajo: ’Azhą́’ ’ákót’ée ndi t’áá naakidi ninááda’siigigo ’anáá’oot’ą́

English: and we hauled wood to the trading post.
Navajo: Naalyéhé bá hooghangi chizh nihiigeeh

English: The main thing we hauled was hay.
Navajo: ’Áko díí tł’ohígíí t’éiyá ’aghá nideiigé
